data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin rose along with US stocks, but the options market still bets on downside risk.
data.description: Bitcoin rose to approximately $74,935 during Asian trading hours, up 0.7% in the past 24 hours and 5.4% for the week. However, the derivatives market is sending mixed signals. QCP Capital points out that this rally is primarily driven by the spot market, rather than a comprehensive recovery in risk appetite. Currently, Bitcoin perpetual contract funding rates remain negative, and open interest is declining, indicating that short sellers are still hedging rather than being forced to close positions. The options market is also cautious: short-term implied volatility is low, the one-month term is less than three months, and risk reversal indicators show that market demand for downside protection is higher than upside bets, suggesting that traders are more willing to pay for potential declines rather than chasing rallies. QCP believes this is more like a "bounce" than a trend reversal. On the macro level, long-term US Treasury yields and gold price movements have not confirmed a recovery in risk appetite; gold remains near high levels, indicating that safe-haven demand still exists. The institution points out that the current market movement is more of a "sentiment repair" driven by ceasefire expectations, rather than a resolution of core risks. Furthermore, Ethereum performed relatively strongly, with the ETH/BTC ratio rebounding to approximately 0.0315. This, coupled with record highs in on-chain transaction volume and stablecoin supply, indicates a potential rotation of funds towards high-beta assets. However, the market still needs to observe the evolution of subsequent risk events to confirm the sustainability of this upward trend. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Machi Big Brother's Crypto Holdings Show Significant Unrealized Profit
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Hyperbot data reveals that Machi Big Brother currently holds long positions in Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and HYPE, with a combined value exceeding $56.5 million. These positions are generating nearly $1.9 million in unrealized profit, indicating a strong performance in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Following Allbirds' overnight surge of nearly 600%, industry insiders have issued a warning: investors should be wary of the risk of "AI whitewashing."
data.description: Allbirds, the "celebrity shoe" brand, announced its bet on the AI ​​infrastructure sector and changed its name to NewBird AI, aiming to compete with CoreWeave by investing in GPU computing power. Its stock price surged nearly 600%, but several industry insiders have issued warnings. Matt Domo, CEO of FifthVantage, stated that Allbirds' AI transformation seems more like a means to boost its weak stock price. Investors should be wary of "AI washing," where some companies attempt to exaggerate or even fabricate their AI capabilities for marketing purposes. Furthermore, there are precedents for companies trying to capitalize on hot trends through aggressive transformations; many companies attempted to ride the blockchain wave in late 2017 and early 2018. Jason Schloetzer, Associate Professor at Georgetown University's McDonough School of Business, pointed out that the initial $50 million funding is "negligible compared to the actual investment required to become such a service provider." However, from a more optimistic perspective, the influx of new players into the AI ​​field may reflect the market's "continued enthusiasm" for growth. Seaport Research analyst Jay Goldberg believes it's hard to imagine Allbirds, a company that entered the field later in life, offering competitive products or services. (Marketwatch)

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Critical Resistance at 100-Day SMA
data.description:
Bitcoin is at a pivotal point, according to BlockBeats. Analyst Alicharts reports that Bitcoin is testing the 100-day simple moving average (SMA) resistance level for the third time in six months. Previous attempts in October and January resulted in failures, leading to significant price drops.

In October, Bitcoin's failure to break through led to a 30% decline from $116,000 to $80,000. Similarly, in January, another failed attempt resulted in a 39% drop from $97,000 to $59,800.

Alicharts warns that a third rejection at this level could signify a major structural failure, potentially triggering a triple top effect and pushing Bitcoin's price back to the annual low of $59,800. Conversely, if Bitcoin manages to break through and close above the 100-day SMA, it could pave the way for a rise to $80,000 – $84,000, indicating that a macro adjustment might be complete.

data.title: Crypto Investor Huang Licheng Increases BTC and HYPE Holdings
data.description:
Crypto investor Huang Licheng has recently expanded his positions in BTC and HYPE, according to Odaily. Hyperbot data reveals that his overall portfolio is showing a floating profit of nearly $1.9 million. 

Huang's holdings include a 25x leveraged ETH position with 13,700 ETH, valued at $32.255 million, and a floating profit of $1.514 million. Additionally, he holds a 40x leveraged BTC position with 202 BTC, valued at $15.117 million, and a floating profit of $175,000. 

Furthermore, Huang has a 10x leveraged HYPE position with 202,000 HYPE, valued at $9.104 million, and a floating profit of $170,600.

data.title: Blockchain Emerges as Operating System for AI Agents
data.description: Blockchain technology is increasingly being recognized as a crucial operating system for AI agents, particularly when these agents require identity verification, payment systems, and authentication. According to PANews, the integration of blockchain provides a secure and efficient framework for AI operations, enhancing their functionality and reliability. This development highlights the growing intersection between blockchain and artificial intelligence, as both technologies continue to evolve and complement each other in various applications.

data.title: Franklin Templeton: Dollar to Remain Dominant Currency
data.description:
On April 16, Jin10 reported that the dominance of the U.S. dollar continues to be a topic of significant interest. According to Franklin Templeton, the dollar is expected to remain the preferred currency. The company's Chief Investment Officer for Fixed Income, Sonal Desai, stated in a report that the dollar's status is supported by three main pillars: the size of the world's largest economy, the depth of its markets, and its institutional credibility. She emphasized that there is currently no reliable alternative asset, and establishing the institutional infrastructure needed to support such a currency would take decades.

The debate over the dollar's dominance has intensified due to U.S. President Donald Trump's unpredictable trade, geopolitical, and fiscal policies, which have diminished the appeal of U.S. assets. Some analysts suggest that the euro and gold could potentially become preferred reserve assets.

data.title: CoW DAO Recovers Domain After Phishing Attack
data.description: CoW DAO reported that on April 14, an attacker used forged documents to seize control of the cow.fi domain, deploying a phishing site. According to NS3.AI, the attack initially involved a wallet drainer and subsequently employed fake wallet pop-ups to extract seed phrases and passwords from users. CoW DAO has since regained control of the domain, ensuring that cow.finance remained operational throughout the incident. The project is now in the process of gradually transitioning users back to cow.fi.

data.title: A senior Trump aide said the U.S. has the capability to continue the blockade of Iranian ports "indefinitely."
data.description: White House advisor and senior Trump aide Stephen Miller stated that the United States has the capability to continue the blockade of Iranian ports "indefinitely" if Iran chooses the wrong path. (Al Jazeera)

data.title: Franklin D. CIO: Alternative assets to the dollar are still decades away.
data.description: The dominance of the US dollar has been a subject of much debate, but Franklin Templeton believes it will remain the preferred currency. Saul Desai, chief investment officer of the firm's fixed income division, wrote in a report that the dollar's position is supported by three pillars: the size of the world's largest economy, market depth, and institutional credibility. Desai points out that there are currently no reliable alternative assets, and building the institutional infrastructure needed to support this currency will take decades. The debate over dollar dominance is intensifying as Trump's erratic trade, geopolitical, and fiscal policies have diminished the attractiveness of US assets. Some analysts believe the euro and gold are poised to become preferred reserve assets. (Jinshi)

data.title: Thailand Seeks Oil and Fertilizer Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
data.description: Thailand is urgently seeking new supplies of oil and fertilizer due to escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, which have depleted its reserves. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's efforts to mitigate the impact of the geopolitical standoff on its energy and agricultural sectors. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments, has become a focal point of international concern, affecting supply chains worldwide. Thailand's government is actively negotiating with alternative suppliers to ensure stability in its domestic markets. The situation underscores the broader implications of regional conflicts on global trade and resource availability.

data.title: PhotonPay Enhances Payment Capabilities with AI-Driven Approach
data.description:
PhotonPay, a stablecoin payment infrastructure provider, has announced a comprehensive upgrade to its payment capabilities, transitioning towards an Agentic Payments paradigm. According to ChainCatcher, this shift focuses on moving from a 'directive' to an 'intent-centric' financial model, enabling AI agents to autonomously plan and execute complex financial pathways.

PhotonPay's system is divided into three main modules: a global network layer, a core execution layer, and an intelligent decision-making layer. AI agents can automatically complete processes such as procurement triggering, payment path optimization, dynamic exchange rate adjustments, and compliance checks, facilitating machine-to-machine (M2M) cross-border fund transfers across over 200 countries and regions, with real-time financial decision support.

Market forecasts by McKinsey suggest that by 2030, the global Agentic Commerce driven by AI agents could reach $3 trillion to $5 trillion, providing significant market validation for PhotonPay's strategic direction.

data.title: BitMart contracts will list IWNONUSDT, WFCONUSDT, IWFONUSDT, and ITAONUSDT.
data.description: According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch perpetual contracts for IWNONUSDT, WFCONUSDT, IWFONUSDT, and ITAONUSDT on April 16, 2026 at 16:00 (UTC+8), supporting leverage up to 20x.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $67.847 million yesterday, marking the fifth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $67.847 million yesterday (April 15th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $31.5064 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.769 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net inflow of $24.7859 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.883 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $13.794 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 4.84%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $11.798 billion.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Concerns Rise in Rural Spain Over Big Tech's Data Center Plans
data.description: Residents in rural Spain are expressing concerns over potential forced land sales and water shortages as major technology companies plan to establish data centers in the region. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the apprehensions of locals who fear the impact of AI-driven developments on their communities. The expansion of data centers, driven by the increasing demand for AI technologies, has raised alarms about the environmental and social consequences. Local communities worry that the construction of these facilities could lead to the depletion of water resources and disrupt traditional land ownership. As AI continues to grow, the balance between technological advancement and community welfare remains a critical issue.

data.title: Middle East Conflict Expected to Raise Japan's Core Inflation Rate, Economists Say
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Reuters reveals that 62% of economists anticipate the ongoing Middle East conflict will lead to an increase in Japan's core inflation rate by 0.2 to 0.4 percentage points over the next 12 months. According to Jin10, this projection reflects concerns about potential disruptions in global supply chains and energy markets, which could impact Japan's economy. Economists are closely monitoring the situation, as any significant changes in inflation could influence monetary policy decisions by Japan's central bank.

data.title: STOCKS | Global Stock Markets Reach Record Highs Amid Iran Tensions
data.description: Global stock markets have reached unprecedented levels despite potential disruptions from the Iran shock. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the unexpected resilience of stocks in the face of geopolitical tensions. Analysts are examining the factors contributing to this market strength, including investor sentiment and economic indicators. The situation continues to evolve, with market participants closely monitoring developments in Iran and their potential impact on global financial stability.

data.title: Cow DAO: The CoW Swap domain was attacked by social engineering and control has now been regained.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that Cow DAO stated on the X platform that attackers gained control of the cow.fi domain on April 14th by forging documents and deceiving the DNS registrar. The attackers deployed a highly realistic phishing website, operating in two phases: first, they tricked users into signing malicious transactions using a wallet stealer; then, they stole mnemonic phrases and passwords through fake wallet pop-ups. Control of the cow.fi domain has now been regained and it has been running normally on cow.finance for some time; the transition back to the original domain is underway.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $186 million yesterday, with BlackRock's IBIT leading the way with a net inflow of $292 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $186 million yesterday (April 15th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $292 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $64.267 billion. The Morgan Stanley ETF (MSBT) followed with a net inflow of $19.3242 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $72.3613 million. Conversely, the Fidelity ETF (FBTC) saw the largest single-day net outflow of $47.3457 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $10.881 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $97.566 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.51%, and a cumulative net inflow of $57.05 billion.
